stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Limnologists are scientists who study inland bodies of water.
  • Some limnologists define a lake as any inland body of standing water.
  • Using that definition, they determined that Finland has roughly 188,000 lakes.
  • Other limnologists require a lake to be at least 1 hectare in surface area and at least 3 meters deep.
  • Using that definition, they determined that Finland has roughly 56,000 lakes.
stem: The student wants to make and support a generalization about limnologists' definition of a lake. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals?

answerOptions.content: Limnologists' competing definitions can yield very different lake counts, as the case of Finland makes clear.
Multiple lake counts for Finland have been produced using different definitions of a lake.
Based on the stricter definition, Finland has roughly 56,000 lakes meeting the size and depth requirements.
The definition of a lake as any inland body of standing water is endorsed by some limnologists, scientists who study inland waters.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it makes a clear generalization (different definitions yield very different counts) and supports it with the Finland example.

Choice B states a fact but doesn't generalize about definitions. Choices C and D give individual data points without making a generalization.
